question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Hi — thanks for the clear description. Your symptoms match a recent helpdesk case where the root cause was a hardware connection issue and the ticket was handled and closed at 1st-level support. What happened in the matching case - The user could hear others, other media (chat/camera) worked, Teams showed the USB headset selected, and the Teams test call recorded nothing — the Teams input level stayed empty
context: SOURCE 37: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Call participants cannot hear the user Ticket number: TI-1616128 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 07 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Telephone Type: Incident Created on: 14.10.25 09:18 Closure Closure date: 14.10.25 09:52 Closure text: Microphone not properly connected. Device configured correctly. Function restored. Activities 14.10.25 09:10 – Telephone report Mr. Contact Person 07 reports that an employee can hear everything during Teams calls but cannot be heard by the other participant. Both internal and external calls are affected. Chat works. Camera works. 14.10.25 09:18 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 14.10.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good morning Could you please briefly confirm: – Is a headset or the integrated microphone being used? – Does the issue also occur during a Teams test call? Thank you. 14.10.25 09:32 – Customer response A USB headset is being used. No sound is recorded during the test call either. 14.10.25 09:40 – Remote session Connected with the user. In Teams, “USB Audio Device” is selected as the microphone. However, the input level indicator remains empty. In the Windows Sound menu, no input signal is displayed either. After a brief check, it was determined that although the USB headset was plugged in, the microphone cable on the headset itself was not fully inserted. After correctly plugging it in, the input level indicator immediately showed activity. Test call performed → Microphone functioning. 14.10.25 09:50 – User feedback Call tested. Working properly again. 14.10.25 09:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 07 The microphone was not properly connected, which prevented any input signal from being detected. After correctly reconnecting the device, voice transmission is functioning normally again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - During a remote session the helpdesk found the microphone cable on the headset itself was not fully inserted; after correctly plugging it in the input meter showed activity and the Teams test call recorded audio. question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - If your headset has a detachable mic cable or an inline connector, make sure that mic cable/plug is fully seated — even if you already unplugged/replugged the USB, the internal mic plug can still be loose; in the example that exact check fixed the issue immediately
